import {
  ColorScales,
  TypographySamples,
  RadiusSamples,
} from "./theme-demo";

export const metadata = { title: "Theme" };

# Theme & Design Tokens

A single stylesheet (`tokens.css`) defines every color, size, and motion value in Patch UI. Components read from it through Tailwind utilities. Light and dark switch from one `.dark` class on `<html>`. No JavaScript required.

The scheme is deliberately small: one canvas, a four-step surface ladder plus an elevated rung for popups, four ink tiers, three hairline steps, three fixed semantic status roles, one monochrome primary. Consumers layer their own brand accent by redefining `--primary` in a single downstream layer.

## Colors

<ColorScales />

**Canvas + surface ladder.** The page sits on `--canvas`. `--surface-1` through `--surface-4` are the successively-lifted rungs (used for hover tints, toggle-group tracks, striped-table fills, skeleton blocks). `--surface-elevated` is the popup + control-chrome rung: it lifts toward pure white in light mode and one step above canvas in dark mode. Every Card, Menu, Combobox, Tooltip, Input, Select, and Table container reads from `--surface-elevated`.

**Ink (text).** Four tiers, deliberately narrow. `--ink` is primary text (headings, body). `--ink-muted` is meta text and default icon tone. `--ink-subtle` is placeholder and "missing value" copy. `--ink-tertiary` is disabled and footnote.

**Semantic status.** `--error`, `--warning`, `--success` are fixed hex, identical in both themes so a warning always reads as a warning. Each has `-hover`, `-active`, and `-fg` variants; the `-fg` token carries label color so fill and text invert together.

**Hairlines.** Three tiers, all rgba-alpha, so borders read cleanly on any surface without hard-coded color. `--hairline` (4%) is the default separator. `--hairline-strong` (8%) is for hovered inputs and structural section dividers. `--hairline-tertiary` (14%) is for nested content that sits on top of an already-lifted surface.

**Primary (monochrome).** `--primary` resolves to `--ink` and `--on-primary` to `--canvas`. No chromatic brand ships. `--primary-hover` and `--primary-active` are computed via `color-mix` toward `--canvas`, so a consumer's brand override on `--primary` automatically produces matching hover/active steps.

## Typography

Three families cover every use: **Inter Display** for display sizes, **Inter** for body / buttons / captions, **JetBrains Mono** for code. Each `text-*` class bakes in size, line-height, tracking, and weight, so you never pair it with a `font-*` utility.

<TypographySamples />

Rules of thumb:
- Never combine a compound `text-*` class with `font-medium` or `font-semibold`. If you need a different weight at a given size, switch families: `text-heading-*` is 600, `text-button-*` is 500, `text-body-*` and `text-caption-*` are 400.
- Never `text-[Npx]` inline. If a size is missing, add it to the scale.
- Never `font-mono` for decoration. Reserve for `code`, `pre`, `kbd`, install snippets, and hex values shown as code.

## Sizing and motion

**Radius.** Four values.

<RadiusSamples />

`radius-6` for controls (Button, Input, Select, Checkbox, Menu row). `radius-12` for surfaces (Card, Dialog, Menu popup). `radius-16` for marketing chrome (hero panels). `radius-full` for chips (Badge, Avatar, pill Button).

**Shadows.** Elevation is flat with 1px hairline borders. Shadows appear only on true overlays: `--shadow-card` (marketing cards), `--shadow-menu` (Menu / Combobox / Select popup), `--shadow-tooltip` (Tooltip), `--shadow-modal` (Dialog / Sheet / Toast).

**Motion.** One easing (`--ease-standard`), two durations: `--duration-state` (75ms, for hover/active transitions) and `--duration-overlay` (150ms, for open/close). Under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, both collapse to `0ms` automatically.

**Focus.** A 2px outline offset 2px outside the element border, colored `--primary` so it inherits any consumer brand override. Delivered through the `focusRing` recipe. Inputs are the intentional exception: on focus their border shifts to `--primary` instead of stacking an outside ring, so the input's own edge carries the state.

## Customizing

Every token is a plain CSS variable. Override in your own CSS after importing the tokens and every component picks up the change at runtime, no rebuild required:

```css
/* app/globals.css */
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "./styles/patch-ui-tokens.css";

/* Ship a lavender brand: every primary Button, active tab, focus
   ring, and slider indicator picks it up automatically. */
:root {
  --primary: #8b5cf6;
  --on-primary: #ffffff;
}

.dark {
  --primary: #a78bfa;
  --on-primary: #0f0f10;
}
```

If you edit tokens that affect readable text, run `npm run check:contrast -w packages/react` to verify the [contrast contract](/docs/contrast) still holds.
